Vocabulary in Context

词汇记忆短文集

在阅读中记忆单词 · 目标词高亮标注 · 点击上方按钮切换

01

第一列

国际贸易博览会 · A Day at the International Trade Fair

37 词
proximity n. 接近；邻近
barren adj. 贫瘠的；荒芜的
radiate v. 辐射；散发
craftsmanship n. 手艺；工艺
testify v. 作证；证明
sentiment n. 情绪；意见
intimidate v. 恐吓；威慑
supposedly adv. 据称；据说
managerial adj. 管理的；经理的
retailer n. 零售商
elite n. 精英；精英阶层
deficit n. 赤字；逆差
supplement n./v. 补充物；增补
graphic adj. 图形的；生动的
bilingual adj. 双语的
momentum n. 动力；势头
debris n. 碎片；残骸
deposit n./v. 押金；存款
expertise n. 专长；专业知识
coast n. 海岸
memorandum n. 备忘录
dietary adj. 饮食的
exotic adj. 异国的；奇异的
modify v. 修改；调整
solitude n. 独处；孤独
isolated adj. 孤立的；偏僻的
variable adj./n. 可变的；变量
shelter n./v. 庇护；避难所
subtle adj. 微妙的；细微的
interpersonal adj. 人际的
intensive adj. 密集的；强化的
profitable adj. 有利可图的
pit n. 坑；深渊
convert v. 转换；改变
bind v. 捆绑；约束
scooter n. 踏板车；小型摩托车
skyscraper n. 摩天大楼

短文阅读 · Essay

The annual International Trade Fair sprawled along the coast, its gleaming pavilions casting reflections that seemed to radiate across the harbor. In close proximity to the city's financial district—where a glass skyscraper dominated the skyline—the fairground had turned a once barren stretch of shoreline into a bustling marketplace. Elena, a negotiator whose bilingual expertise in cross-cultural deals was widely respected, arrived early on her scooter, weaving past the debris of the previous night's celebration.

Inside the main hall, stalls displayed goods that celebrated craftsmanship from every continent: hand-woven textiles, exotic spices in tidy rows, and a dietary supplement promising renewed vigor. An elite group of importers circled the displays, among them a prominent retailer searching for profitable new lines to stock. For many exhibitors, however, the season's trade deficit had forced them to supplement declining domestic sales with foreign contracts.

Elena's role was largely managerial. She drafted a memorandum outlining terms, supervised a substantial deposit on a container shipment, and worked to convert cautious interest into signed contracts that would bind both parties for the year. As the morning wore on, momentum built steadily. A few exhibitors tried to intimidate rivals with flashy demonstrations, yet the prevailing sentiment favored quiet, interpersonal trust over spectacle.

Throughout the afternoon, Elena observed how the fair could testify to globalization's reach—or, as critics supposedly claimed, to its excesses. Graphic posters advertised how manufacturers modify their lines to suit variable consumer tastes. In one corner, a noisy pit of auctioneers shouted bids beneath a temporary shelter that offered only subtle relief from the sun. She found the pace intensive but exhilarating, and when the crowds thinned, she savored a rare moment of solitude—feeling, for a few minutes, wonderfully isolated from the world's clamor.

02

第二列

实验室突破 · Laboratory Breakthrough

35 词
alumnus n. 校友
tribe n. 部落；族
irate adj. 愤怒的
algorithm n. 算法
parliament n. 议会；国会
irritated adj. 恼怒的；发炎的
expansion n. 扩张；膨胀
autonomy n. 自治；自主权
dependent adj. 依赖的
array n. 一系列；阵列
sanctuary n. 避难所；圣所
consensus n. 共识；一致
census n. 人口普查
hypothesis n. 假设；假说
portray v. 描绘；扮演
exceed v. 超过；超出
herd n. 兽群；牧群
entrepreneur n. 企业家
rear n./v. 后部；抚养
monarch n. 君主
payroll n. 工资名单；薪金总额
quota n. 配额；定额
compensate v. 补偿；赔偿
bizarre adj. 奇异的；古怪的
multiply v. 乘；繁殖；增加
correlate v. 相关；关联
foster v. 培养；促进
genetically adv. 基因上地
mineral n. 矿物
scheme n./v. 计划；图谋
simultaneously adv. 同时地
invention n. 发明
dean n. 院长；教务长
supervisor n. 主管；导师
deck n. 甲板；层面

短文阅读 · Essay

When Dr. Owen Hart returned to his alma mater as a distinguished alumnus, the chemistry laboratory was buzzing with excitement. His former supervisor, now gray-haired but sharp-eyed, greeted him beside a cluttered deck of instruments, while the dean herself had cleared her afternoon to witness the demonstration. The team had been running a new algorithm that processed data from a genetically modified mineral sample, and the preliminary results seemed to validate their central hypothesis—an invention, they believed, that could revolutionize materials science. Two experiments had run simultaneously overnight.

Yet the atmosphere was not entirely harmonious. A small tribe of junior researchers felt irritated by the relentless pace, and one irate technician accused a colleague of cutting corners. The mood grew bizarre when an entrepreneur who had funded part of the project arrived unannounced, demanding to know whether the lab's payroll could sustain the next quarter and whether the team would meet its promised quota. Hart, anxious to compensate for the tension, ushered everyone toward the rear of the lab for coffee.

The breakthrough, he explained, carried implications far beyond the university. Parliament had recently debated regulations on such research, and even the monarch had expressed curiosity about its economic potential. Hart argued that scientific autonomy must not become dependent on commercial whims; unchecked expansion without consensus could be dangerous. A national census of research output, he noted, showed that few institutions could exceed international benchmarks, and the data did not correlate funding with genuine innovation.

In a quiet sanctuary at the lab's heart, Hart urged his hosts to foster collaboration rather than competition. He wanted to portray science as a shared pursuit, one in which discoveries multiply benefit for all—not a herd of rivals quick to scheme for advantage. As he surveyed the vast array of equipment, he felt certain that this small room, modest and cluttered, might yet change the world.

03

第三列

北极探险危机 · Arctic Expedition Crisis

32 词
gross adj. 总的；严重的
owe v. 欠；归功于
trigger v. 触发；引发

adverse adj. 不利的；相反的
prolong v. 延长；拖延
epidemic n. 流行病；传染病
prestigious adj. 有声望的
predator n. 捕食者
polar adj. 极地的；两极的
antibiotic n. 抗生素
ladder n. 梯子
stimulus n. 刺激；激励
intolerance n. 不宽容；偏执
harsh adj. 严酷的；刺耳的
dismiss v. 驳回；不予理会
accustomed adj. 习惯的
interpretation n. 解释；诠释
exert v. 施加；努力
devise v. 设计；发明
subsidy n. 补贴；津贴
cater v. 迎合；提供饮食
surgeon n. 外科医生
resilience n. 韧性；恢复力
resurrection n. 复活；复兴
tenant n. 租户；佃户
dropout n. 辍学者
lever n. 杠杆
outsource v. 外包
scenario n. 设想；情景
deteriorate v. 恶化
depleted adj. 耗尽的；枯竭的

短文阅读 · Essay

The polar research station stood defiant against the harsh Arctic wind, where ice was abundant but mercy was scarce. Dr. Lena Voss, leader of a prestigious expedition, had grown accustomed to adverse conditions—yet nothing in her training had prepared her for what came next.

It began when a starving predator, a polar bear driven inland by melting floes, seemed ready to trigger the camp's perimeter alarm. In the chaos that followed, a technician fell from a maintenance ladder and fractured his leg. The team's surgeon, working by flashlight, cleaned the wound and administered the last antibiotic in their stores, fearing that any infection could spark an epidemic in such close quarters. The crew had to exert themselves hauling the injured man to shelter.

By the third day, conditions began to deteriorate. Food supplies were depleted, and the radio's batteries were dying. Lena knew the sponsor would owe them a resupply that never came; back home, through gross negligence, officials were quick to dismiss the crisis as exaggerated, their bureaucratic intolerance threatening to prolong every decision. She refused to panic.

Drawing on her resilience, she began to devise a plan for the worst scenario: to ration what remained, to outsource nothing, and to cater only to survival. Then word crackled through that a government subsidy had been approved—a lever and stimulus that would fund a rescue flight. Among her crew was a former dropout who proved unexpectedly resourceful, and a tenant farmer turned mechanic whose practical interpretation of the broken engine saved them all. When the rescue helicopter lifted them from the ice, Lena felt as though she were witnessing a resurrection—life returning to a place that had seemed determined to claim them.

04

第四列

王国新政 · Kingdom's New Policy

27 词
agenda n. 议程；事项
tactic n. 战术；策略
threshold n. 门槛；界限
substitute n./v. 替代品；替代
prospective adj. 预期的；潜在的
livestock n. 牲畜
aspirin n. 阿司匹林
signify v. 表示；意味着
fiscal adj. 财政的
fixture n. 固定物；常驻事物
deterioration n. 恶化；退化
degradation n. 退化；降级
recreation n. 娱乐；消遣
depict v. 描绘
affluent adj. 富裕的
testimony n. 证词；证据
exceptional adj. 杰出的；异常的
ideology n. 意识形态
profile n. 形象；侧面
indulge v. 纵容；沉溺
bias n. 偏见；偏心
indispensable adj. 不可或缺的
steep adj. 陡峭的；急剧的
acre n. 英亩
confine v. 限制；禁闭
slash v. 大幅削减；砍
intimate adj. 亲密的；私下的

短文阅读 · Essay

The kingdom of Valdoria had long been affluent, its rolling fields of grain and herds of livestock stretching across every fertile acre. Yet recent years had brought a steep decline: fiscal mismanagement, the slow deterioration of public trust, and the environmental degradation of once-rich farmland. Queen Maris understood that the realm had crossed a dangerous threshold, and that half-measures would no longer suffice.

Her reform agenda was ambitious. She rejected the rigid ideology of the old court, refusing to let bias confine her choices, and she moved to slash wasteful spending from the royal budget. Advisors she had once considered indispensable warned that such sweeping change was reckless, but Maris believed only exceptional leadership could save the kingdom. Her chief tactic was transparency.

To signify a new era, the queen commissioned artists to depict scenes of ordinary life rather than royal vanity, raising the public profile of farmers and merchants. She courted prospective allies among the regional lords, offering fair trade in place of the favors with which her predecessors would indulge cronies. Even royal recreation was reined in; the hunting parks were opened to commoners, and the queen shared intimate conversations with villagers she once might never have met. Where once a substitute for honesty had been the court's currency, now truth prevailed.

The transformation did not come easily. Cynics demanded testimony of real results before they would believe. Yet within a decade Valdoria had become a fixture of stability in a turbulent region. On the evening the final reform passed, Maris sat alone with a cup of tea and a single aspirin for her headache, smiling at the thought that a kingdom she had nearly lost now stood renewed.

01

第一列

不懈求真 · Relentless Pursuit of Truth

25 词
municipal adj. 市政的；地方的
realm n. 领域；范围
handicapped adj. 残疾的；有障碍的
relentless adj. 不懈的；残酷的
quest n. 探索；寻求
postpone v. 推迟；延期
wipe v. 擦拭；消除
deficiency n. 缺乏；不足
mortality n. 死亡率；必死性
intensity n. 强烈；强度
domain n. 领域；领地
era n. 时代；年代
occasional adj. 偶尔的；不时的
chord n. 和弦；弦
ignorance n. 无知；愚昧
laborious adj. 费力的；艰苦的
disclose v. 揭露；透露
suppress v. 压制；抑制
plough v. 犁地；艰难前行
hermitage n. 隐居处；修道院
nuisance n. 讨厌的人/物；麻烦事
dim adj. 昏暗的；模糊的
fluctuation n. 波动；起伏
monopoly n. 垄断；独占
legislator n. 立法者；议员

短文阅读 · Essay

In every era, the relentless quest for truth has defined humanity's highest calling. Dr. Elena Hart devoted her life to this pursuit, retreating to a quiet hermitage beside the municipal library where she could think without interruption. She believed that the domain of knowledge belonged to everyone, not held in monopoly by the privileged few who treated facts as private property.

Her research focused on mortality rates in working-class districts, a laborious task that required her to plough through decades of crumbling records. She refused to postpone her findings despite pressure from a powerful legislator who hoped to suppress the truth and protect his allies. The data revealed a deficiency in public sanitation that left entire neighborhoods handicapped for a generation, and she felt compelled to disclose it whatever the cost.

The intensity of her work was matched only by the occasional doubt that crept in during the long nights. In the dim light of her study, she would strike a chord on her old piano to steady her nerves and clear her mind. Ignorance, she insisted, was the true nuisance — not the fluctuation of public opinion or the noise beyond her window.

When her findings finally entered the realm of policy debate, they challenged every comfortable assumption. She wiped away the excuse that nothing could be done, that the past was beyond repair. Her relentless spirit proved that one person, armed with patience and evidence, could confront a hidden crisis and demand a reckoning from those in power.

02

第二列

边境小镇危机 · Border Town Crisis

30 词
maturity n. 成熟；完备
sewage n. 污水；污物
civic adj. 市民的；公民的
eligible adj. 合格的；有资格的
illuminate v. 照亮；阐明
treaty n. 条约；协议
moderate v. 缓和；节制
prevalent adj. 普遍的；流行的
barrel n. 桶；桶装物
catastrophic adj. 灾难性的
enrolment n. 注册；登记
hostile adj. 敌对的；不友善的
seize v. 抓住；夺取
toll n. 通行费；代价
prescription n. 处方；药方
ferry n. 渡船；渡运
predominantly adv. 主要地；占主导地
inferior adj. 劣等的；下级的
grim adj. 严酷的；阴郁的
enforcement n. 执行；实施
finite adj. 有限的
sequence n. 顺序；连续
collide v. 碰撞；冲突
cosy adj. 舒适的；惬意的
prairie n. 大草原
paradox n. 悖论；矛盾
evolutionary adj. 进化的；演变的
stunning adj. 令人震惊的；极好的
merchant n. 商人
paradise n. 天堂；乐园

短文阅读 · Essay

The town of Ridgemont sat on the edge of the prairie, a once-cosy settlement that travelers had called paradise. Now it faced a grim reality. Sewage overflow had become prevalent, and the predominantly aging population struggled with civic decline. A local merchant named Tomasz watched the catastrophic damage unfold with the maturity of someone who had survived worse.

The town's ferry crossing — its only lifeline — drew from a finite supply of fuel, and tensions began to collide as two rival factions fought over the remaining supplies. Enforcement of the old treaty had collapsed. The sequence of failures was stunning in its speed. Each barrel of contaminated water exacted a toll that no prescription could cure.

Tomasz sought to moderate the conflict, but hostile voices would seize every opportunity to escalate. He understood the paradox: a community that prided itself on harmony was now its own worst enemy. The evolutionary pressure of hardship revealed who people truly were beneath the surface.

Eligible residents saw enrolment in the regional aid program as their last hope, yet the assistance proved inferior to what had been promised. To illuminate the path forward, Tomasz rallied his neighbors, insisting that even paradise could be rebuilt if people chose cooperation over conflict.

03

第三列

都市梦 · Urban Dream

30 词
cricket n. 蟋蟀；板球
frustration n. 挫折；沮丧
certify v. 证明；认证
glamorous adj. 迷人的；富有魅力的
fluid adj. 流动的；流体的
aspire v. 渴望；立志
metropolitan adj. 大都市的
competence n. 能力；胜任
cholesterol n. 胆固醇
dose n. 剂量；一剂
crawl v. 爬行；缓慢行进
inertia n. 惰性；惯性
uncover v. 揭露；发现
subsistence n. 生存；维持生活
coordinate v. 协调；配合
evident adj. 明显的；显然的
regulatory adj. 监管的；规范的
pledge n./v. 保证；誓言
precedent n. 先例；前例
glamour n. 魅力；诱惑力
aggravate v. 加重；恶化
indigenous adj. 本土的；原住民的
chronically adv. 长期地；慢性地
sphere n. 球体；领域
millennium n. 千年；千禧年
constraint n. 约束；限制
relocate v. 重新安置；搬迁
cumulative adj. 累积的
catastrophe n. 灾难；大祸
utterly adv. 完全地；彻底地

短文阅读 · Essay

For as long as she could remember, Amara aspired to leave behind the slow rhythm of her village — where the only sound at dusk was the song of a cricket — and chase the glamorous life she saw in magazines. She longed to enter a sphere where ambition was fluid and the glamour of the metropolitan skyline promised infinite possibility.

But the new millennium brought its own constraints. The city, she discovered, was chronically indifferent. Rent devoured her wages until she lived at subsistence level, and the inertia of poverty made every morning feel like a crawl. Her frustration grew. A doctor warned her that stress and a poor diet had raised her cholesterol; she needed a daily dose of medication just to function.

The regulatory maze of urban life could aggravate even the smallest problem. To certify her credentials, she had to coordinate with three agencies, each one setting a precedent for delay. It was evident that the system favored those already inside. Still, Amara made a pledge to uncover a path forward. She could not let the cumulative weight of setbacks become a catastrophe.

Then came an opportunity to relocate near an indigenous arts collective that celebrated rather than erased her roots. The move utterly transformed her. Her competence finally shone. The dream had not been a mirage — only a road that demanded resilience.

04

第四列

企业诉讼 · Corporate Lawsuit

commodity n. 商品；日用品

designate v. 指定；指派
liability n. 责任；负债
segregation n. 隔离；分离
elimination n. 消除；淘汰
supplier n. 供应商
strategic adj. 战略的；策略的
jury n. 陪审团
syndrome n. 综合征；症候群
misfortune n. 不幸；灾祸
expedition n. 探险；远征
sue v. 起诉；控告
invasive adj. 侵入的；入侵的
confrontation n. 对抗；冲突
drone n. 无人机；雄蜂
certification n. 认证；证明
retrieve v. 取回；检索
underlie v. 构成…的基础
affiliate n. 附属机构；分支机构
alleviate v. 减轻；缓解
pasture n. 牧场；草地
spur v. 刺激；激励
default n./v. 违约；默认
spectacular adj. 壮观的；惊人的
hint n. 暗示；线索
grave adj. 严重的；庄严的
monetary adj. 货币的；金融的

短文阅读 · Essay

The corporation had begun its endeavour with the best of intentions — a strategic partnership to extract a rare commodity from the highland pasture. But misfortune struck when an invasive drilling technique began to impair the water table. Local farmers, whose livelihoods underlie the region's economy, sued the company. They sought monetary compensation and the elimination of the offending operation.

The confrontation was spectacular in its scale. A drone captured footage of the damage, and the evidence was grave. The company's lead affiliate tried to retrieve documents that might alleviate the blow, but a whistleblower's hint exposed what they hoped to hide. The syndrome of corporate denial was familiar: designate a scapegoat, suppress dissent, and default to denial.

In court, the jury listened as lawyers tried to quantify the harm. The segregation of the affected communities from clean water was undeniable. A supplier testified that the certification of safety had been falsified. The expedition to gather evidence had taken months, and now it would spur a landmark ruling.

The judge noted that no settlement could fully restore the land, but the ruling would confirm the company's liability. What had gone wrong was not a single mistake but a culture that treated nature as expendable. The endeavour to rebuild trust, the judge said, must begin with honesty.

01

第一列

星海探微 · A Researcher's Galactic Discovery

32 词
persist v. 坚持；持续
adversary n. 对手；敌手
assume v. 假定；假设；承担
endorse v. 赞同；认可；代言
intimacy n. 亲密；密切关系
galactic adj. 银河的；星系的
intuitive adj. 直觉的；凭直觉的
skeptical adj. 怀疑的；持怀疑态度的
assert v. 断言；坚称；主张
conducive adj. 有助于…的；有益的
inevitably adv. 不可避免地；必然地
formulate v. 构想；制定；系统阐述
stake n. 赌注；利害关系；股份
provision n. 供应；预备；规定
irrational adj. 不理性的；无理性的
dawn n. 黎明；开端
bulk n. 大部分；体积；大块
miniature adj. 微型的；缩小的
graze v. 擦过；掠过；吃草
ingenuity n. 独创性；心灵手巧；足智多谋
exploitation n. 开发；利用；剥削
retreat v. 撤退；退避
embed v. 嵌入；植入；使深留
distort v. 扭曲；歪曲；使失真
deem v. 认为；视为
publicity n. 宣传；公众关注
pyramid n. 金字塔；棱锥体
sculpture n. 雕塑；雕刻品
tilt v. 倾斜；翘起
illiterate adj. 文盲的；不会读写的
stray v. 迷路；偏离
dividend n. 股息；红利；回报

短文阅读 · Essay

At the dawn of her career, Lina refused to let senior colleagues assume her ideas were merely intuitive guesses. Her chief rival, Dr. Mercer—a formidable adversary—would assert in every meeting that her theories were irrational, and would deem her unorthodox methods hardly conducive to real science. Yet Lina would persist, convinced that the bulk of the evidence supported her vision.

She worked in a modest hilltop observatory where a miniature telescope, its aging mount quick to tilt in the wind, was all she had. Colleagues, deeply skeptical of her ambition, watched her stray from accepted research paths. They warned that the exploitation of such limited equipment would inevitably distort her data. But Lina, whose grandmother had been illiterate yet possessed an intimacy with the night sky that credentialed scholars envied, trusted that ingenuity could outshine privilege.

To formulate her hypothesis, she had to embed fresh calculations into old star maps. Each observation let faint comet-trails graze the edges of her charts like the carved lines of a sculpture, or the layered steps of a pyramid rising toward truth. She made provision for every possible error, refusing to retreat when funding stalled and advisers withdrew. The stake was enormous: if she was right, she would reveal a hidden galactic current invisible to far larger instruments.

Months passed with little publicity. Then, one frozen morning, the data aligned at last. Lina could finally endorse her own conclusion—a ribbon of dark matter threading the galaxy, exactly where her models had pointed. What critics once deemed fantasy now paid a generous dividend of recognition, and universities rushed to endorse her work.

02

第二列

心灵重启 · Rediscovering the Essence of Life

34 词
presumably adv. 大概；据推测
essence n. 本质；精髓
entail v. 需要；使成为必需
disruption n. 扰乱；中断
curve n. 曲线；弧线

psychiatrist n. 精神科医生
outperform v. 胜过；表现优于
exaggeration n. 夸张；夸大
feat n. 壮举；功绩

intrigue v. 激起好奇心；引起兴趣
excel v. 擅长；突出
verge n. 边缘
protagonist n. 主角；主人公
explicit adj. 明确的；清楚的
carbohydrate n. 碳水化合物
hectic adj. 忙乱的；繁忙的
legitimate adj. 合理的；合法的
strand n. 一股；一缕
satire n. 讽刺；讽刺作品
timber n. 木材；木料
dwell v. 细想；居住（常与 on 连用）
redundant adj. 多余的；冗余的
crude adj. 粗糙的；粗略的
imminent adj. 迫近的；即将发生的
blur v. （使）变模糊
desperate adj. 绝望的；拼命的
thorough adj. 彻底的；详尽的
calcium n. 钙
imperative adj. 必要的；迫切的
refuge n. 避难所；庇护
probe v. 探查；探究
propel v. 推进；推动

短文阅读 · Essay

Dr. Halloran, a seasoned psychiatrist, studied Elena—the protagonist of this quiet clinical drama—who sat on the verge of collapse. Her hectic schedule had become a devastating force; sleep and meals had eroded until the days would blur into one another, each indistinguishable from the last, draining her further. It was no exaggeration to say she was desperate.

"It is imperative," he said gently, leaning forward, "that we probe what your life actually entails, not merely what you fear it might."

Elena had come seeking refuge from an imminent breakdown. Presumably, she believed therapy would be a crude patch—a redundant ritual she could simply outlast before returning to her old routines. Instead, Dr. Halloran's thorough questions began to intrigue her, pulling her toward an honesty she had long avoided.

He did not dwell on symptoms alone. He asked about her diet—was she getting enough calcium, enough carbohydrate to fuel a body that still tried to excel at work? She had tried to outperform every colleague by skipping meals, a legitimate but misguided strategy that only sharpened the downward curve of her energy.

Rather than issue explicit commands, he wove small metaphors. "Think of your resilience as a single strand of timber," he said. "One beam can hold, but a roof needs many." She smiled, sensing the mild satire in his tone—he was gently mocking the myth that one person, alone, could accomplish every feat.

Through weeks of quiet advocacy for her own well-being, Elena learned to propel herself forward, one small choice at a time. The disruption of old habits was painful at first, yet it gave her room to breathe and to notice what had been lost. Slowly, she rediscovered the essence of living: not constant achievement, but balance, rest, and the quiet courage to ask for help.

03

第三列

寻根之旅 · An Archaeologist's Expedition

autonomous adj. 自治的；自主的

rein n. 缰绳；控制权

compatible adj. 兼容的；相容的

factual adj. 事实的；基于事实的

revive v. 使复苏；使复兴
burial n. 埋葬；葬礼
jeopardise v. 危及；损害
repository n. 贮藏室；储藏库
reckon v. 估算；认为；想
dedication n. 奉献；献身；专注
notably adv. 显著地；尤其
circulate v. 流传；循环；散布
incur v. 招致；引起；承担
deliberate adj./v. 蓄意的；深思熟虑的
compulsory adj. 强制的；必修的；义务的
drift v./n. 漂流；漂移；飘动
rigour n. 严格；严密；严酷
haul n./v. 拖拉；搬运；长途运输
nominate v. 提名；任命
violation n. 违反；违背；侵犯
fraud n. 欺诈；骗局；骗子
slack adj. 松弛的；懈怠的；马虎的
descendant n. 后代；后裔
aisle n. 过道；通道；走廊

短文阅读 · Essay

Dr. Elena Voss decided to embark on an expedition to trace her ancestry, hoping to uncover evidence of the ancient people from whom she, a proud descendant, had inherited her restless curiosity. As the only autonomous researcher funded by her institute, she kept a firm rein on every detail of the project, refusing to let her standards grow slack.

She drove her automobile across the desert, her hands locked in a tight grip on the wheel as the vehicle narrowly avoided a collision with a fallen boulder. The conception of this journey had taken years to mature, rooted in her dedication to scholarly rigour and a desire to revive a forgotten chapter of history.

At the site, her team began the long haul of excavation. Elena had to detach herself from wishful expectations and examine every chunk of compacted soil with deliberate care. It was compulsory for each worker to log every finding; any violation of protocol could jeopardise the entire dig. Rumours soon began to circulate among the crew—some would speculate that the tomb was a hoax, even a fraud—but Elena insisted on factual evidence rather than hearsay.

Notably, the team's methods proved compatible with international standards, and the resilient archaeologists weathered sandstorms that would have undone a lesser expedition. When they cleared the narrow aisle leading into the chamber, the significance of their discovery began to manifest: rows of clay tablets, a buried repository of ancient knowledge, preserved through centuries of burial. Elena could only reckon that her ancestors had hidden this archive on purpose to protect it.

The team would later nominate the site for heritage protection, and the careful reproduction of the tablets' inscriptions would allow scholars worldwide to study them. Elena knew the expedition might incur enormous costs, yet as she watched the evening wind drift across the dunes, she felt certain that her journey had honored those who came before her.

04

冰川危途 · A Village Transcends Its Fate

stigma n. 耻辱；污名
plague n./v. 瘟疫；虫害；折磨

pesticide n. 杀虫剂；农药
rationality n. 理性；合理性
implicit adj. 含蓄的；暗含的
immense adj. 巨大的；广大的
aggregate n./adj. 总计；聚集物；合计的
therapeutic adj. 治疗的；有疗效的
stance n. 立场；姿态
shatter v. 粉碎；砸碎；使破灭
scattered adj. 散落的；分散的

短文阅读 · Essay

The cottage village of Aelvik huddled at the foot of an iconic glacier whose meltwater, season after season, carved the valley's sole road. Remote and proud, the settlement clung to this harsh corner of the world as though the ice itself had granted it permission to exist. For generations the villagers' quiet tenure felt unshakeable—until a plague of invasive bark beetles drifted in on the warming tide of glacial runoff, scattered across every slope and crevice.

Within months the infestation grew notorious throughout the highlands. The insects proved susceptible to only one pesticide, yet spraying the saturated wetlands was both tedious and hazardous, demanding masks, long hoses, and days of exhausting labor. Dead timber soon piled into an immense aggregate of brittle wood, and each wind-borne moan through hollow trunks sounded like the forest drawing its last breath. Fear left the village council paralysed; rationality crumbled into suspicion, and an implicit stigma settled on anyone who dared whisper of abandoning the valley.

Yet the spectacle of collapsing pines began to contradict the elders' stubborn stance that the mountain would always shelter its own. Younger villagers, restless and grieving, wanted to rebel—to shatter old customs and leverage modern science before the beetles consumed everything. But tradition would oblige no such easy surrender, and the quarrels grew bitter enough to split families.

It was Mara, the forester, who finally offered a compact remedy: blend the chemical defense with a therapeutic replanting of native birch, whose bitter bark the beetles disdained. Patient by patient, acre by acre, the dying slopes might heal, and with them the courage of a frightened people. Only by marrying old endurance with new knowledge, she argued, could Aelvik transcend its grim fate and silence the forest's grief forever—proving that even catastrophe can be met, not with panic, but with stubborn, careful hope.

05

第五列

街区新生 · A Community Builds a New Future

magnitude n. 重要性；规模
medieval adj. 中世纪的
contempt n. 蔑视；轻视
provoke v. 激起；挑衅
diesel n. 柴油
additive n. 添加剂
warrant v. 值得；证明……正当
kidney n. 肾脏
embody v. 体现；代表
viable adj. 可行的
edible adj. 可食用的
flaw n. 缺陷；瑕疵
drain n./v. 排水沟；消耗；排空
spin v. 旋转；歪曲叙述
conversion n. 转换；改造
vigorous adj. 有力的；精力充沛的
bust v. 打破；使破产
lament v. 哀叹；惋惜
eradicate v. 根除；消灭
resemble v. 类似；相似
discard v. 丢弃；抛弃
likelihood n. 可能性
dispose v. 处理；处置
consultation n. 咨询；磋商
inferior adj. 劣质的；次的
veteran n./adj. 老兵；经验丰富的人
applicable adj. 适用的；可应用的
sneak v. 偷偷地走；潜行
predictable adj. 可预测的
import v./n. 进口；引入

短文阅读 · Essay

For decades, the medieval quarter of our town lay in ruins, a symbol of deprivation that many outsiders viewed with contempt. The magnitude of the decay seemed too vast to warrant serious attention, and city officials would often lament the district's slow decline while doing nothing. Only one plausible solution remained: the community itself had to renovate the area from within, block by block.

A veteran builder named Marta led the effort. She refused to discard the original stonework, insisting that every restored wall could embody the district's lost dignity. During early consultation meetings, skeptics argued the plan wasn't viable, claiming the cost would drain public funds for years. Yet Marta's vigorous advocacy began to provoke genuine interest among younger residents. She pointed out the one flaw in every objection: nobody had actually tried.

Volunteers set to work. They cleared rubble, fixed a broken drain, and transformed a derelict warehouse through conversion into a community kitchen serving edible meals from salvaged produce. To safely dispose of hazardous waste, including old diesel drums and chemical additive containers, they followed regulations applicable to industrial cleanup sites. Sometimes children would sneak in to watch the progress, their excitement a predictable sign of changing attitudes.

The results began to resemble a real neighborhood again. A small clinic opened, even offering kidney health screening for elderly residents. The likelihood of the district's revival, once unthinkable, now seemed certain. Marta refused to let officials import cheap, inferior materials that would undercut the work. She argued that morality demanded they build honestly, not just cheaply. To bust the cycle of poverty, the community had to eradicate the assumption that poor neighborhoods deserve poor craftsmanship.

When critics tried to spin the project as a failure, the evidence spoke otherwise. What had been ruins now stood as homes, gardens, and gathering spaces—a future built by hand.

01

街区复兴 · A District Fights Bankruptcy to Find Renewal

dread n. 恐惧；畏惧
modernize v. 使现代化

fraction n. 小部分；一点儿
bruising adj. 艰苦的；激烈的
overstate v. 夸大；言过其实
duplicate adj. 复制的；完全相同的
revelation n. 揭示；惊人的发现
specialty n. 特色食品；特产
affection n. 喜爱；感情
tame v. 驯服；制服；整治
primitive adj. 原始的；粗犷的
tranquility n. 宁静；平静
irrigate v. 灌溉
staple adj. 主要的；基本的
frantic adj. 慌乱的；狂乱的
badge n. 徽章；象征
scoop n. 独家新闻；独家报道
corrosive adj. 腐蚀性的；破坏性的
marginal adj. 边缘的；微不足道的
nominal adj. 象征性的；名义上的
preliminary adj. 初步的；预备的
bureaucracy n. 官僚主义；官僚机构
instruction n. 指示；说明
bankruptcy n. 破产
dazzle v. 使目眩；使倾倒
spacious adj. 宽敞的
layoff n. 裁员；解雇
endorsement n. 认可；支持；背书
bleak adj. 黯淡的；荒凉的

短文阅读 · Essay

The bleak factories along Riverbend's canals stood as monuments to a vanishing age, and residents watched their savings evaporate as the district slid toward bankruptcy. After the latest layoff at the textile mill, a frantic despair settled over the streets, and the dread of total collapse became a daily companion. Shops shuttered, families packed, and hope seemed to drain away with each passing week.

Yet not everyone surrendered. Mara Voss, a conscientious urban planner who had grown up among these streets, refused to overstate the district's chances but also refused to abandon it. She secured a nominal grant from city hall—barely a fraction of what was truly needed—only after surviving a bruising battle with bureaucracy. Every form required duplicate copies; every permit demanded explicit instruction from three separate departments. The corrosive effect of such delays had already killed a dozen promising projects, but Mara pressed on.

Her preliminary plan was deceptively simple: modernize the crumbling waterfront, irrigate the abandoned greenhouses, and convert them into a lively market where vendors could sell local specialty foods. The centerpiece would be a spacious community hall designed to dazzle visitors and draw tourism back to the district. To tame the overgrown lots, she hired former factory workers—men whose rough hands still moved with primitive strength but who held a deep, stubborn affection for their neighborhood.

The breakthrough revelation came when a young journalist got the inside scoop: the plan had quietly won the endorsement of a retired governor. Almost overnight, marginal skeptics turned into eager supporters. Workers wore their new responsibilities like a badge of pride, and a staple crop of fresh herbs and greens filled the greenhouse shelves.

Within two years, Riverbend found a fragile tranquility. It was not prosperity—not yet—but the district had proven that renewal could take root even in exhausted soil.

02

海岛守卫 · A Coastal Nation Resists Embargo

assault n. 袭击；攻击
embargo n. 禁运；贸易封锁
envisage v. 设想；预见
gale n. 大风；狂风
restrain v. 抑制；克制

allure n. 诱惑力；魅力
ordeal n. 煎熬；严酷考验
allege v. 声称；断言
furious adj. 猛烈的；狂怒的
deduction n. 推断；演绎
turmoil n. 动荡；混乱
credential n. 资历；凭据
unleash v. 释放；发动
fallacy n. 谬论；谬误
undernourished adj. 营养不良的
cereal n. 谷物
whip v. 驱使；鞭策
evade v. 躲避；规避
ingenious adj. 巧妙的；独创的
ardently adv. 热烈地；热切地
irony n. 讽刺；反讽
bolster v. 支撑；增强

短文阅读 · Essay

When the hostile empire declared an embargo on the island republic of Marindel, few could envisage the ordeal that would follow. Warships began to patrol the harbors, and a sudden assault on the coastal batteries left many homes vacant, their families fled inland. The invaders allege that Marindel had provoked the conflict, but this was a fallacy woven from propaganda.

The irony was bitter: Marindel had long been famous for its hospitality toward foreign merchants. Now, with trade severed, the economy fell into a slump, and the people grew undernourished on meager rations of cereal and dried fish. The turmoil seemed insurmountable. Yet the islanders refused to surrender; they ardently believed their freedom had intrinsic worth beyond any treaty's merit.

General Lira, whose credential as a strategist was unmatched, understood that durable resistance required ingenious tactics rather than brute force. She reorganized the militia's orientation toward guerrilla defense, using the rocky coves and gale-swept channels to evade the enemy's superior firepower. When the empire tried to whip its troops into a furious offensive, Lira's fighters would strike supply lines and vanish into the fog.

To bolster morale, the resistance council distributed a simple printed brochure outlining survival strategies and rationing schedules—a small document that became integral to every household. Citizens learned to restrain their fear, and through calm deduction of intercepted messages, they predicted each new attack.

The invaders believed they could unleash enough pressure to break the republic's spirit. They could not comprehend the allure that liberty held for a people who had tasted it. Season after season, through storm and hunger, Marindel endured. When the empire finally agreed to enact a ceasefire, the world recognized that courage and cleverness had prevailed. The island stood scarred but sovereign—a testament that resilience, when rooted in purpose, can outlast any siege.

03

第三列

艺术寻觅 · An Artist Seeks Eternal Truth Through Carpentry

31 词
depiction n. 描绘；描写
wither v. 枯萎；凋谢
startling adj. 令人吃惊的
eternal adj. 永恒的
crave v. 渴望
divert v. 转移
blunt adj. 钝的；不锋利的
penetrate v. 穿透；透过
insulate v. 隔离；使隔绝
parameter n. 参数；界限
reimburse v. 偿还；报销
surrender v. 屈服；投降
hamper v. 阻碍；妨碍
stagnation n. 停滞
overlap n. 重叠；交集
swamp v. 淹没；使应接不暇
recede v. 退去；消退
avert v. 避开；转移
utter adj. 完全的；彻底的
sensational adj. 轰动的；耸人听闻的
rehearsal n. 排练；预演
casualty n. 受害者；牺牲品
haunt v. 困扰；萦绕
rivalry n. 竞争；对抗
carpentry n. 木工；木匠活
offspring n. 产物；后代
discern v. 辨别；识别
ditch v. 抛弃；丢弃
brisk adj. 轻快的；利落的
peninsula n. 半岛
discrepancy n. 差异；不一致

短文阅读 · Essay

The artist Marcus lived on a windswept peninsula, where the sea's roar could not insulate him from the memories that haunt his every waking hour. For years, a bitter rivalry with his former mentor had left him paralyzed in creative stagnation, his once-vivid canvases reduced to gray smears. He would watch his inspiration wither like autumn leaves, unable to penetrate the wall of self-doubt that surrounded him.

Marcus came to crave something beyond the sensational praise of critics, something more eternal. He decided to divert his energy into carpentry, trading brushes for chisels. The brisk rhythm of shaving wood felt honest, each stroke a quiet rehearsal for a greater masterpiece. He set a strict parameter for his new work: every piece must discern truth from vanity.

Yet old habits refused to recede. The overlap between his two crafts — the depiction of life in wood and in paint — produced a startling result. His carvings began to capture emotion his paintings never could, as if the blunt grain of oak could reach the viewer's heart more deeply than any pigment. He refused to surrender to the temptation of easy fame, avoiding the utter collapse that had claimed his mentor as its first casualty.

Critics tried to swamp him with commissions, but he would avert their gaze and ditch the shallow projects they proposed. When a gallery owner offered to reimburse him handsomely for quick, market-friendly pieces, Marcus noticed a sharp discrepancy between what sold and what mattered. The offspring of his solitude — a series of carved wooden figures — spoke louder than any canvas.

His work would not hamper the spirit with ornament; it stripped life to its bones. And in that simplicity, Marcus finally found what he had sought: not victory over a rival, but the quiet truth that outlasts applause.

04

第四列

孤岛寻芳 · A Botanist's Solitary Expedition

35 词
brisk adj. 轻快的；凛冽的
consciousness n. 意识；知觉
surplus n. 过剩；剩余
score n. 二十（计数量词）
flatter v. 奉承；讨好
spectator n. 旁观者；观众
craze n. 狂热；风潮
upbringing n. 抚养；教养
rationing n. 配给；定量供应
lump n. 肿块；硬块
renowned adj. 著名的
uplift n. 振奋；提升
friction n. 摩擦；冲突
liable adj. 易于…的；可能…的
populate v. 居住于；占据
soothe v. 安慰；抚慰
mansion n. 宅邸；大厦
worldly adj. 世俗的；尘世的
garment n. 衣服；服装
proclaim v. 宣称；宣告
torture n. 折磨；酷刑
fleet n. 船队；舰队
attain v. 获得；达到
solitary adj. 孤独的；独自的
complement v. 补充；补足
dissertation n. 学位论文
kidnap v. 绑架；劫持
roam v. 漫游；徜徉
obscure adj. 鲜为人知的；晦涩的
stripe n. 条纹；狭长地带
magnetic adj. 有磁性的；有吸引力的
credibility n. 可信度；信誉
botanist n. 植物学家
injection n. 注射
caption n. 说明文字；标题

短文阅读 · Essay

The renowned botanist Dr. Elena Voss adjusted her weathered garment against the brisk wind sweeping across the remote peninsula. She had come alone on this solitary expedition, seeking a rare medicinal plant whose magnetic pull on her consciousness had grown unbearable.

A score of years had passed since she last stood on this shore. As a child, her upbringing in a crumbling mansion had been marked by wartime rationing and a surplus of silence. Her father would proclaim that science was the only path to attain truth, yet he watched her ambitions with the cold detachment of a spectator. That rejection had formed a hard lump in her chest, a quiet torture that no achievement could soothe.

Now, hiking paths where few humans populate the landscape, she let her thoughts roam as freely as the seabirds overhead. A small fleet of fishing boats dotted the horizon, but she barely noticed. Her mind was fixed on the obscure flower said to grow only along one rocky stripe of cliff. Finding it would validate her life's work, her dissertation, her very credibility.

She recalled how, years ago, a media craze had tried to flatter her into celebrity. She had refused every interview, knowing that praise was liable to breed complacency. There had been friction with colleagues who accused her of arrogance, but she ignored them. What she sought was not admiration but the quiet uplift of discovery.

When at last she spotted the pale blue petals clinging to the stone, tears stung her eyes. This plant, she believed, could complement modern medicine, offering relief where a synthetic injection failed. No one would kidnap this knowledge for profit—not if she had anything to say about it. She photographed the flower and wrote a careful caption beneath each image, documenting the moment for posterity.

She knelt beside the flower, breathing slowly. In that moment, the worldly ambitions that had once weighed her down seemed distant. She had not escaped her past, but she had outgrown its grip.

01

第一列

制药风暴 · A Researcher Races Against a Perilous Epidemic

neutral adj. 中立的；不偏不倚的
harness v. 利用；驾驭
creep v. 悄悄蔓延；潜行
crossbreed v. 杂交；交叉培育
successive adj. 连续的；接连的
sightseeing n. 观光；游览
submarine n. 潜水艇
elevation n. 海拔；高处
formation n. 结构；形成物
revival n. 复苏；复兴
molecular adj. 分子的
banquet n. 宴会

trim v. 修剪；整理
stalk v. 悄悄跟踪；纠缠
cocaine n. 可卡因
cultivation n. 培养；栽培
ritual n. 惯例；仪式
buzz n. 嗡嗡声；喧闹
comply v. 遵从；服从
nominee n. 被提名人
pharma n. 制药（业）
pharmacist n. 药剂师
wary adj. 警惕的；小心的
recipient n. 接受者；受药者
peril n. 危险；危难
dwarf v. 使相形见绌；使显得渺小
counsel n. 建议；忠告

短文阅读 · Essay

Dr. Lena Voss, a dedicated pharma researcher and former pharmacist, stared at the molecular models lining her lab. The epidemic's successive waves had brought the city to the edge of ruin, and every chronicle of the outbreak grew darker. The peril was undeniable; bodies filled hospital corridors while panic began to creep through every neighborhood.

Lena's daily ritual was to review the cultivation of viral cultures under the microscope, searching for a formation she might harness to build a vaccine. The lab's buzz of activity felt almost like hyperactivity, technicians rushing between benches. To clear her head, she would sometimes step outside and gaze at the elevation of the distant hills—no time for sightseeing, only brief respites.

The path was treacherous. Some colleagues, reckless in their ambition, suggested they deceive oversight committees to accelerate trials. A fellow nominee for the research prize urged her to crossbreed two viral strains in ways that could elicit dangerous mutations. Lena remained wary, seeking counsel from her ethics mentor before any decision.

The disease's traumatic toll haunted her: survivors described agony that would dwarf any suffering they had known, worse even than cocaine withdrawal. She could not comply with shortcuts that might endanger a future recipient of the drug. Like a submarine surfacing into light, her conscience broke through the pressure.

She kept an atlas of the outbreak pinned to her wall, plotting where case spikes would coincide with supply shortages. Each morning she would trim the data, discarding noise. Rumors began to stalk her—that she was hoarding the formula. At a tense banquet honoring frontline workers, a reporter tried to corner her, but Lena stayed neutral, refusing to fuel speculation.

The revival of hope came when trials finally succeeded. That night, Lena chose to unplug her phone, sit in silence, and let herself believe the worst was over.

02

第二列

贵族觉醒 · An Aristocrat Resigns Privilege for Solidarity

expenditure n. 支出；花费
loom v. 隐约浮现；逼近
lure n. 诱惑；吸引力

vicious adj. 凶残的；恶毒的
orthodox adj. 正统的；传统的
portrait n. 肖像；画像
idealistic adj. 理想主义的
turbulent adj. 动荡的；骚乱的
solidarity n. 团结
extinguish v. 熄灭；扑灭
renovation n. 翻修；改造
soak v. 浸透；渗透
trickle v. 滴流；缓缓渗入
eccentric adj. 古怪的；异乎寻常的
hygiene n. 卫生
confer v. 商讨；交谈
manuscript n. 手稿；原稿
stall v. 拖延；使停顿
dessert n. 甜点
descend v. 走下；下降
aloof adj. 冷漠的；超然的
drawback n. 缺点；不利之处

短文阅读 · Essay

The portrait on the wall showed a young aristocrat in tailored silk—born to descend a grand staircase each morning, aloof from the hunger gnawing below. Yet something in that painted smile concealed an idealistic restlessness no inheritance could quiet.

After finishing his postgraduate studies in political philosophy, he watched privation trickle through the city slums while lords casually debated expenditure on banquets and hunts. The contrast became a drawback of his birth he could no longer ignore. He decided to resign his title, knowing the choice was irreversible.

His father, a proud patron of orthodox tradition, refused consent. "You would perish out there," he warned, predicting a vicious takeover by lawless mobs. But the young man would not let fear stall his conscience. He packed a single carton of belongings and slipped away at dawn.

Beyond the frontier, a turbulent movement was rising—farmers and laborers demanding equity and solidarity against corrupt landlords. Clouds of revolution seemed to loom over every village, yet the cause acted as a lure he could not resist.

Conditions were grim. Hygiene was poor, meals were thin, and the only dessert was a shared apple passed among comrades. An eccentric old printer let him edit a hand-set manuscript calling for fair wages. Together they would confer late into the night, while the damp began to soak through their thin blankets.

During the renovation of an abandoned barn into a meeting hall, he learned to carry timber like anyone else. He watched comrades extinguish their lamps at midnight, rise at dawn, and labor without complaint. Privilege, he discovered, could be set aside—just as a flame could be snuffed—once the will to serve burned brighter.

He never returned to the manor. The frontier had claimed him, not by force but by conviction.

03

第三列

沙漠迷途 · A Carefree Merchant's Miraculous Escape

33 词

disposable adj. 一次性的；可丢弃的

aversion n. 厌恶；反感
sin n. 罪恶；罪过

jeopardy n. 危险；危难
scepticism n. 怀疑；怀疑态度
contemplate v. 沉思；深思
compile v. 收集；编纂
preferable adj. 更可取的；更合意的
fabricate v. 编造；捏造
detriment n. 损害；不利
carefree adj. 无忧无虑的
ventilate v. 使通风；使空气流通
optimal adj. 最佳的；最适宜的
restless adj. 烦躁不安的；坐立不安的
prerequisite n. 先决条件；前提
outbreak n. 爆发；突然发生
lane n. 小巷；窄路
vacancy n. 空缺；空位
maim v. 使残废；使受重伤
malnutrition n. 营养不良
plight n. 困境；苦境
divine adj. 神圣的；神的
irresistible adj. 不可抗拒的；无法抵挡的
miraculous adj. 奇迹般的；不可思议的
prevalence n. 普遍；盛行
inanimate adj. 无生命的；无生气的
reluctance n. 不情愿；勉强

短文阅读 · Essay

Once a carefree merchant named Elias traveled with a caravan across the desert, trading spices and silks in a lucrative business. His masculine frame and confident stride made him a natural leader. He shrewdly bargained at every market, turning even disposable trinkets into profit.

One evening, as the caravan entered a narrow lane between sandstone cliffs, bandits ambushed them. The outbreak of violence was sudden—arrows whistled, threatening to maim anyone in range. Several guards fell, and Elias was captured. The bandits dragged him to a fortress to imprison him indefinitely.

His cell had no window to ventilate it, and the stale air worsened his restless nights. Food was scarce; soon malnutrition set in, to the detriment of his health. His plight seemed hopeless. The jailers, with cruel aversion to prisoners, offered only scraps. When a fellow captive died, the vacancy in the next cell went unfilled for weeks.

Elias began to contemplate his fate. He tried to compile fragments of memory—prayers his mother had taught him, stories of divine deliverance. He felt an irresistible urge to pray, though scepticism whispered that no one was listening. Yet something stirred within him, not in the inanimate stones around him, but in his own spirit.

The prevalence of suffering in the prison was evident; many inmates showed reluctance even to speak. But Elias refused to fabricate despair. He believed hope was a prerequisite for survival, and that dignified silence was preferable to complaint. He gathered the prisoners and called for a vote; the ayes were unanimous—they would dig.

Working in jeopardy of discovery, they carved a tunnel with improvised tools. The optimal moment came during a sandstorm that muffled all sound. What followed was nothing short of miraculous: they emerged free beneath a sky blazing with stars.

Elias had entered the desert a carefree trader. He left it knowing that sin and cruelty could chain the body, but never the soul.

04

傀儡觉醒 · A Puppet Successor Yearns for Truth

reign v. 统治；盛行

ubiquitous adj. 无处不在的；普遍存在的
brutal adj. 残酷的；野蛮的
synthetic adj. 合成的；人造的
stifle v. 压制；扼杀
inventory n. 清单；详细目录

intuition n. 直觉

短文阅读 · Essay

In a brutal kingdom where fear and superstitious ritual reign supreme, the old tyrant lies dying. His chosen successor, Prince Aldric, is widely regarded as a mere puppet, a figurehead installed to preserve the malignant order that has viciously crushed dissent for decades. Yet beneath his compliant exterior, Aldric harbors a secret resolve: he has come to resent the cruelty done in his name and cannot help but yearn to assimilate the truth the regime has buried beneath layers of synthetic propaganda.

The kingdom runs on an assortment of manufactured doctrines, each a module of control designed to interweave loyalty with terror. The premise is simple but absurd: that the ruler's bloodline is synonymous with divine favor, and any doubt is attributable to demonic influence. This contention is reinforced through a circuit of priests who patrol every village, ensuring that ubiquitous shrines dominate each vista of the landscape. To question them is to risk the abolition of one's family name.

Aldric knows the word "reform" carries a dangerous connotation here. Any hint of change would polarise the court, and his advisors urge him to stifle such impulses before they take root. Yet his intuition tells him the people are ready. Whispers gather spontaneously in the marketplaces, and letters arrive from offshore exiles describing freer lands governed by a written constitution. He must not let the river of truth grow muddy with half-measures.

Through deep immersion in forbidden archives, Aldric catalogues every facet of the regime's crimes. He compiles a careful inventory of injustices, preparing for the day he can dismantle the machinery of oppression and forge a just future.

01

defective adj. 有缺陷的
auditory adj. 听觉的

rust n./v. 锈；生锈
expend v. 花费；消耗
malfunction n./v. 故障
refinement n. 精炼；改进
hypocritical adj. 伪善的
vicinity n. 附近
genre n. 类型；流派
crunchy adj. 松脆的
perpetual adj. 永久的
inspector n. 检查员
concede v. 承认；让步
suffice v. 足够

短文阅读 · Essay

When Inspector Morrison arrived at the five-storey hospital, she could already sense that something was wrong. The building seemed to sprawl across the entire block, and in the vicinity of the east wing, a perpetual hum of anxiety filled the air. She had come to investigate a defective auditory implant, whose malfunction had harmed three patients and caused detrimental effects to their hearing. The gigantic manufacturer, known for its fertile innovations in medical devices, now faced a breach of safety regulations that no polite explanation could suffice to explain.

Morrison took a sip of cold coffee and began to articulate her questions to the hospital staff. She had to enlist the help of a forensic engineer to determine whether rust on the device's slender components had contributed to the failure. The aftermath of the incident had wreaked havoc on patients' lives; some could no longer enjoy even the simple pleasure of a crunchy apple without pain. One elderly man sat in his bed, his gaze fixed on the window, too weary to mourn the silence that now surrounded him.

The company's representative was hypocritical, offering a confession only after Morrison threatened to expose the scandal. He was forced to concede that the refinement process had been rushed and that quality controls had been ignored. His conscience, it seemed, was overdue in surfacing. "We cannot expend any more lives for the sake of a profitable genre of medical gadgets," Morrison declared, her voice savage with controlled anger.

She noted that the textile used to wrap the devices was substandard and that the hospital's own inspector had failed to test the auditory modules thoroughly. It was clear that the company had valued profit over safety, and the investigation would continue until every detail was accounted for.

02

美术馆之谜 · Mystery at the Art Gallery

profoundly adv. 深刻地
indifference n. 冷漠
expel v. 驱逐；开除

obedient adj. 顺从的
anecdote n. 轶事

inspect v. 检查
discourse n. 论述；交谈
commend v. 表扬
distortion n. 扭曲；变形

短文阅读 · Essay

The Crestview Art Gallery was a place that seemed to radiate elegance. VIP guests received preferential treatment, sipping wine while admiring the exhibits. On the night of the annual exhibition, however, a disastrous event unfolded. The gallery's most prized trophy, a golden sculpture, was found with a hairline fracture across its base, a distortion of its original form that no superficial repair could hide.

Curator Lena, a woman of calm disposition, stood poised at the entrance, her pale complexion betraying only the slightest alarm. She had taken every precaution: security cameras, guards, and locked display cases. Yet someone had managed to toss the sculpture to the floor with startling stupidity. "Who would do such a thing?" she could not help but exclaim.

The prime suspect was a juvenile visitor, a teenager known for his unorthodox behavior and his indifference to rules. He had been seen lingering near the exhibit, sharing an anecdote about how he wanted to emulate famous art vandals. The guards, obedient to protocol, had promptly escorted him to a back room, but they could not expel him without proof.

Detective Marsh arrived to inspect the scene. He was proficient in art crimes and had a benign manner that put witnesses at ease. He began a discourse with the staff, hoping to commend whoever had acted responsibly. He would ponder the evidence: a small accessory, a dropped earring, lay near the damaged trophy.

The truth, when it emerged, was profoundly surprising. It was not the teenager but a toddler, wandering unseen, who had bumped the pedestal and let the sculpture slam to the floor. The case was closed, and the gallery learned that even the best precautions can fail.

03

校园竞赛丑闻 · Campus Competition Scandal

worship v./n. 崇拜
solemn adj. 庄严的
escalate v. 升级
interval n. 间隔
scrutiny n. 审查

moist adj. 潮湿的

短文阅读 · Essay

The campus art competition had always been a sovereign tradition, a celebration where students could immerse themselves in creativity and showcase their versatile talents. This year, however, the event was ripe for scandal. The winning entry, an extravagant painting of an aquatic scene, was supposed to be an icon of excellence. But during the interval between judging and the awards ceremony, a rumor began to escalate that the work was not original.

Professor Lin, known for her solemn demeanor and sharp scrutiny, decided to barge into the studio to investigate. What she found would baffle the entire faculty: a salient defect in the portrayal of the fish, which bore a striking resemblance to a published photograph. Under careful examination, a faint remnant of a watermark was visible, moist with fresh overpainting. The discovery would scrape away the artist's reputation in an instant.

The student, confronted, could only sketch a vague defense. His argument was mediocre at best, and his claim that the work celebrated the integration of Eastern and Western aesthetics rang hollow. "You treat this competition like a delicacy to be consumed, not a craft to be honored," Lin declared. She refused to worship false talent.

The surveillance footage from the studio confirmed the cheating. The student had tried to remain inconspicuous, but the cameras captured everything. The committee, offering complimentary praise to honest contestants, decided to bar the cheater from future events. The scandal proved a potent lesson: true art cannot be faked, and even the barren landscape of dishonesty yields consequences. Lin later visited the campus clinic, where students practiced acupuncture and other therapies to relieve stress, and paused to praise an honest young painter whose work was genuinely his own.

04

extravagance n. 奢侈
disparity n. 差距
diagnosis n. 诊断
sanitation n. 卫生

短文阅读 · Essay

The old factory on the edge of town had long been a place of peculiar rumors. Its proximity to the river made it damp, and the poor sanitation inside had, hitherto, gone unreported. When Inspector Cole received a mandate to investigate, he approached the case with an unbiased mind, determined to discern the truth behind the workers' complaints.

Inside, a cluster of rusty machines stood in silent rows. The disparity between the owners' extravagance and the workers' misery was immediately discernible. Cole could not align such indulgence with the physiological toll the labor took on the employees. One worker, indebted to the company for housing, confessed that fumes from the vats would occasionally ignite, and that the management's denial of any danger was a trivial lie meant to consolidate their control.

The situation seemed to stump even the experienced inspector. A vague verse, scratched into a wall, read like a warning: "The mighty furnace never sleeps." Cole heard a sharp whistle escape from a cracked pipe, and he felt a surge of fury at the negligence. He would hasten to file his diagnosis of the factory's ills.

The owners tried to refute his findings and to dub him a troublemaker. They argued that the novelty of new equipment would solve everything, and that any alteration to the schedule was unnecessary. But Cole refused to eject the evidence from his report. He demanded their resignation if conditions did not improve, insisting that basic compatibility between profit and human safety was non-negotiable.

In the end, the factory was shut down, and Cole's report became a landmark case in industrial reform.

01

第一列

民众运动 · People's Movement

inhibit v. 抑制；阻碍
linger v. 逗留；徘徊
grind v. 磨碎；苦干
huddle v. 挤作一团；蜷缩
withhold v. 扣留；拒绝给予
forfeit v. 丧失；没收

短文阅读 · Essay

The seeds of rebellion had been sown long before the first shots echoed through the capital. For decades, the people had endured a rigid regime that demanded conformity in every aspect of life. The monotony of daily existence wore down even the most patient citizens, until the collective grind of poverty and oppression became unbearable. It was a recurring pattern throughout the nation's history: a government drunk on its own rhetoric, blind to the suffering beneath it.

When the uprising finally came, it was not premature. The timing rested on a single catalyst — a landmark court ruling that exposed decades of malpractice at the highest levels. The verdict threatened to overturn the entire power structure, and the ruling elite scrambled to dissolve parliament before the news could spread. They attempted to withhold the information from the public, but the truth had already begun to flow through every artery of the nation's communication network.

The regime's response was swift and brutal. Soldiers were ordered to escort opposition leaders out of the city under armed guard, and anyone who resisted faced the threat of slaughter. The military tried to cripple the resistance by severing supply lines, hoping to inhibit any organized counterattack. Yet the rebels refused to forfeit their dream of freedom. In dark cellars and hidden compartments beneath old houses, they would huddle together, imbibe whatever courage they could from shared stories and whispered plans.

The success of the revolution would hinge on one question: could the rival factions reconcile their differences? The tangled web of alliances and old grudges made unity seem like a lofty ideal. Some leaders tried to offset past grievances with promises of reform, while others continued to smuggle weapons across the border, defying every sanction the regime imposed.

As the old order began to ascend toward irreversible collapse, the people watched with a mixture of hope and fear. The smell of smoke would linger in the streets for weeks. Neighbors tried to console those who had lost loved ones, and the new leaders worked tirelessly to reassure a frightened populace that better days lay ahead.

02

第二列

侦探迷案调查 · Detective's Mystery Investigation

35 词
summon v. 传唤；召唤
strangle v. 勒死；扼杀
counterfeit adj. 伪造的；假冒的
endow v. 赋予；资助
reciprocal adj. 互惠的；相互的
unveil v. 揭示；揭幕
mediate v. 调解；斡旋
deplore v. 谴责；强烈反对
trauma n. 创伤；精神创伤
invert v. 倒置；反转
ascribe v. 归因于
prototype n. 原型；雏形
exempt adj. 免除的；豁免的
brink n. 边缘
disperse v. 驱散；分散
repress v. 压制；抑制
impetus n. 动力；推动力
noteworthy adj. 值得注意的
besiege v. 围困；包围
devour v. 吞食；毁灭
retort v. 反驳；回嘴
intelligible adj. 可理解的；明了的
stumble v. 绊倒；踉跄
rectify v. 纠正；修复
reputed adj. 被认为的；有名的
revolt n. 反叛；厌恶
conspiracy n. 密谋；共谋
contagious adj. 传染性的
rehearse v. 排练；预演
groan v. 呻吟；叹息
eminent adj. 杰出的；显赫的
denounce v. 谴责；告发
negligent adj. 疏忽的；粗心大意的
negligible adj. 微不足道的；可忽略的
stagger v. 蹒跚；使震惊

短文阅读 · Essay

Detective Harlan Voss was eminent in his field, a man whose reputation had been endowed by years of solving cases that baffled lesser minds. So when the commissioner decided to summon him to headquarters at midnight, Voss knew the matter was grave. A reputed banker had been found dead in his study — the kind of crime that could strangle a city's confidence in its own institutions.

The evidence pointed toward a conspiracy of remarkable scope. Hidden in the victim's safe were bundles of counterfeit currency and a prototype printing plate still warm from the press. Whoever killed the banker wanted to repress the truth, but the murder itself became the impetus for an investigation that would unveil far more than anyone expected. Voss followed a trail of accomplices, each more negligent than the last in covering their tracks. A single negligible fingerprint on a wine glass proved enough to crack the case wide open.

His prime suspect was a man on the brink of financial ruin. Under interrogation, the man would retort with barely intelligible excuses, then groan as each alibi collapsed under scrutiny. Voss did not deplore the suspect's desperation — he had seen enough trauma to understand what ruin could drive a person to. But he refused to let sympathy invert his judgment. The facts were noteworthy: three witnesses, a reciprocal exchange of encrypted messages, and a motive one could reasonably ascribe to greed.

As the investigation deepened, reporters began to besiege the precinct, hungry for a scandal that would devour the front pages. The panic was contagious; even veteran officers seemed to stagger beneath the weight of public scrutiny. The chief tried to mediate between the press and the department, but every effort to disperse the crowds failed. Critics were swift to denounce the force, and murmurs of revolt rippled through the streets.

Voss, however, would rehearse his final move with meticulous care. He knew the mastermind believed himself exempt from consequence, shielded by wealth and influence. But when the detective stumbled upon a hidden ledger — the one document that could rectify every fabricated lie — the case was sealed. Justice, long delayed, had finally arrived.

03

第三列

中世纪城堡攻防 · Medieval Castle Clash

18 词
intricate adj. 复杂的；精细的
mortal adj. 致命的；凡人的
enclose v. 围绕；圈起
affiliated adj. 附属的；有关联的
sturdy adj. 坚固的；强壮的
humiliate v. 羞辱；使丢脸
propagate v. 繁殖；传播
propaganda n. 宣传
scramble v. 攀爬；争夺
reproach v. 责备；指责
perplexing adj. 令人困惑的
bewildered adj. 困惑的；不知所措的
stagnate v. 停滞；不景气
outrageous adj. 蛮横的；骇人听闻的
snatch v. 夺取；抢走
counteract v. 抵消；对抗
assassinate v. 行刺
dubious adj. 怀疑的；靠不住的

短文阅读 · Essay

The siege of Dunmore Castle had entered its fortieth day, and the defenders were beginning to stagnate in their despair. The walls, though sturdy, could not hold forever against the intricate war machines that the besieging army rolled into position each dawn. King Aldric's engineers had designed siege towers of terrifying complexity, each one built to enclose dozens of soldiers within walls of iron-bound timber. The king's heralds continued to propagate tales of inevitable victory, flooding the countryside with propaganda designed to break the defenders' will.

Inside the keep, Lord Cadwen stood bewildered by the sudden turn of events. Only weeks earlier, his affiliated bannermen had sworn oaths of reinforcement — yet none had arrived. It was a perplexing betrayal, and the lords who remained loyal could only reproach one another for the catastrophic failure. Some whispered that Cadwen should surrender before the king sought to humiliate him publicly, parading him in chains before the entire realm. Others considered it outrageous to even speak of yielding while the banner still flew.

Cadwen refused to let fear counteract his resolve. When a traitor attempted to snatch the keys to the inner gate, he was seized and executed at dawn. Rumors that the king planned to assassinate Cadwen personally had circulated for days, though the sources were dubious at best. Still, the threat of mortal combat hung over every decision made within those stone walls. Whenever the ramparts trembled, soldiers would scramble to reinforce the breaches, knowing that a single failure could doom them all.

学习建议：建议先通读短文，根据上下文推测加粗彩色词的含义，再对照上方词卡复习。每篇短文反复阅读 2–3 遍，有助于在语境中牢固记忆单词。点击顶部按钮可在第一份至第七份之间切换。
